docs.title: User Login Visual
docs.text:
Expand all Collapse all **Design References** Dashboard Login Error State Login Page As a designer I want the login page to maintain its visual appearance So that UI changes are caught before they reach production Scenario: Login page matches visual baseline@smoke Given I am on the login page 14ms FAILED 14ms 
Error: page.goto: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED at http://localhost:3000/login
Call log:
  - navigating to "http://localhost:3000/login", waiting until "load"

   at tests/acceptance/steps/user-login.steps.ts:17

  15 |
  16 | Given('I am on the login page', async ({ page }) => {
> 17 |   await page.goto('/login');
     |              ^
  18 |   await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/login/);
  19 | });
  20 |
Then the page should match the visual baseline "login-page" SKIPPED Scenario: Login error state matches visual baseline Given I am on the login page 11ms FAILED 11ms 
Error: page.goto: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED at http://localhost:3000/login
Call log:
  - navigating to "http://localhost:3000/login", waiting until "load"

   at tests/acceptance/steps/user-login.steps.ts:17

  15 |
  16 | Given('I am on the login page', async ({ page }) => {
> 17 |   await page.goto('/login');
     |              ^
  18 |   await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/login/);
  19 | });
  20 |
When I enter email "invalid" SKIPPED And I enter password "wrong" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then the page should match the visual baseline "login-error-state" with masked: SKIPPED selector .timestamp .dynamic-content Scenario: Dashboard matches visual baseline after login Given a registered user exists with email "testuser@example.com" 318ms FAILED 318ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/test/seed-user
    - user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/147.0.7727.15 Safari/537.36
    - accept: */*
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - content-type: application/json
    - content-length: 82
And I am on the login page SKIPPED When I enter email "testuser@example.com" SKIPPED And I enter password "ValidPassword123!" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then I should be redirected to the dashboard SKIPPED And the page should match the visual baseline "dashboard" with masked: SKIPPED selector .timestamp .user-avatar .notification-count

docs.title: User Login
docs.text: Expand all Collapse all **Design References** Dashboard Login Error State Login Page As a registered user I want to log in to the application So that I can access my dashboard and protected resources Background: Given a registered user exists with email "testuser@example.com" Scenario: Successful login with valid credentials@smoke Given I am on the login page SKIPPED When I enter email "testuser@example.com" SKIPPED And I enter password "ValidPassword123!" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then I should be redirected to the dashboard SKIPPED And I should see a welcome message containing my name SKIPPED Scenario: Login fails with invalid password Given I am on the login page SKIPPED When I enter email "testuser@example.com" SKIPPED And I enter password "WrongPassword" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then I should see an error message "Invalid email or password" SKIPPED And I should remain on the login page SKIPPED Scenario: Login fails with non-existent email Given I am on the login page SKIPPED When I enter email "nobody@example.com" SKIPPED And I enter password "AnyPassword123" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then I should see an error message "Invalid email or password" SKIPPED Scenario: Login validation for empty fields Given I am on the login page SKIPPED When I enter email "<email>" SKIPPED And I enter password "<password>" SKIPPED And I click the login button SKIPPED Then I should see a validation error for "<field>" SKIPPED emailpasswordfield ValidPassword123email testuser@example.compassword email

docs.title: User Crud
docs.text:
Expand all Collapse all As a service consumer I want to manage users via the REST API So that user data is consistently created, read, updated, and deleted Scenario: Create a new user@smoke When I send a POST request to "/api/users" with body: 313ms FAILED 313ms 
      {
        "name": "James Kim",
        "email": "james@example.com",
        "role": "admin"
      }
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 63
Then the response status should be 201 SKIPPED And the response body should contain "id" SKIPPED And the response body "name" should equal "James Kim" SKIPPED And the response body "role" should equal "admin" SKIPPED Scenario: Retrieve an existing user by ID Given a user exists with name "Lookup User" and email "lookup@example.com" 313ms FAILED 313ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 91
When I send a GET request to "/api/users/{userId}" SKIPPED Then the response status should be 200 SKIPPED And the response body "name" should equal "Lookup User" SKIPPED And the response body "email" should equal "lookup@example.com" SKIPPED Scenario: Update an existing user Given a user exists with name "Old Name" and email "update@example.com" 311ms FAILED 311ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 88
When I send a PUT request to "/api/users/{userId}" with body: SKIPPED 
      {
        "name": "New Name"
      }
Then the response status should be 200 SKIPPED And the response body "name" should equal "New Name" SKIPPED Scenario: Delete an existing user Given a user exists with name "Delete Me" and email "delete@example.com" 310ms FAILED 310ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 89
When I send a DELETE request to "/api/users/{userId}" SKIPPED Then the response status should be 204 SKIPPED Scenario: Create user with duplicate email returns 409@negative Given a user exists with name "Existing" and email "duplicate@example.com" 313ms FAILED 313ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 91
When I send a POST request to "/api/users" with body: SKIPPED 
      {
        "name": "Another",
        "email": "duplicate@example.com",
        "role": "user"
      }
Then the response status should be 409 SKIPPED And the response error code should be "EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS" SKIPPED Scenario: Create user with missing required fields returns 400@negative When I send a POST request to "/api/users" with body: 315ms FAILED 315ms 
      {
        "name": "No Email User"
      }
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3000/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 24
Then the response status should be 400 SKIPPED And the response error code should be "VALIDATION_ERROR" SKIPPED Scenario: Retrieve non-existent user returns 404 When I send a GET request to "/api/users/non-existent-id" 310ms FAILED 310ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.get: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3000
Call log:
  - → GET http://localhost:3000/api/users/non-existent-id
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
Then the response status should be 404 SKIPPED And the response error code should be "USER_NOT_FOUND" SKIPPED

docs.title: Cross Service Order
docs.text:
Expand all Collapse all As a system I want orders to propagate correctly across user, order, and inventory services So that data remains consistent across all microservices Background: Given the user service is available at USER_SERVICE_URL And the order service is available at ORDER_SERVICE_URL And the inventory service is available at INVENTORY_SERVICE_URL Scenario: Successful order decreases inventory@smoke Given a user "Order Buyer" exists in the user service 311ms FAILED 311ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3001
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3001/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 101
And product "WIDGET-001" has 50 units in inventory SKIPPED When I create an order for user with 3 units of "WIDGET-001" SKIPPED Then the order service should return status 201 SKIPPED And the order should have status "pending" SKIPPED And eventually the inventory for "WIDGET-001" should be 47 units SKIPPED Scenario: Order rejected for non-existent user When I create an order for non-existent user "ghost-user-id" with 1 unit of "WIDGET-001" 310ms FAILED 310ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3002
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3002/api/orders
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 76
Then the order service should return status 400 SKIPPED And the order error code should be "INVALID_USER" SKIPPED Scenario: Order rejected when insufficient inventory Given a user "Low Stock Buyer" exists in the user service 312ms FAILED 312ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3001
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3001/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 109
And product "RARE-001" has 2 units in inventory SKIPPED When I create an order for user with 5 units of "RARE-001" SKIPPED Then the order service should return status 409 SKIPPED And the order error code should be "INSUFFICIENT_INVENTORY" SKIPPED Scenario: Order status updates propagate to user service@eventual-consistency Given a user "Status Watcher" exists in the user service 313ms FAILED 313ms 
Error: apiRequestContext.post: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:3001
Call log:
  - → POST http://localhost:3001/api/users
    - user-agent: Playwright/1.59.1 (arm64; macOS 26.2) node/23.3
    - accept: application/json
    - accept-encoding: gzip,deflate,br
    - Content-Type: application/json
    - content-length: 107
And product "WIDGET-002" has 100 units in inventory SKIPPED When I create an order for user with 1 units of "WIDGET-002" SKIPPED And the order is marked as "shipped" SKIPPED Then eventually the user service should show the order as "shipped" SKIPPED

docs.title: Rag Quality
docs.text:
Expand all Collapse all As a system owner I want the RAG pipeline to retrieve relevant context and generate grounded answers So that users get accurate, source-backed responses Scenario: RAG answer is faithful to retrieved context@faithfulness Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the retrieved context: PASSED 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      According to the IPCC report, the economic cost of climate inaction is
      estimated at $23 trillion by 2050. In comparison, implementing recommended
      mitigation strategies would cost approximately $4 trillion, making proactive
      action significantly more cost-effective.
Then the faithfulness score should be >= 0.7 8332ms FAILED 8332ms 
Error: OpenAI API key is not set. Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or add `apiKey` to the provider config.
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:166

  164 |
  165 | Then('the faithfulness score should be >= {float}', async ({}, _threshold: number) => {
> 166 |   const result = await matchesFactuality(
      |                  ^
  167 |     ragCtx.question,
  168 |     ragCtx.retrievedContexts.join('\n'),
  169 |     ragCtx.ragAnswer,
Scenario: RAG answer is relevant to user question@relevancy Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the retrieved context: 1ms PASSED 1ms 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      According to the IPCC report, the economic cost of climate inaction is
      estimated at $23 trillion by 2050. In comparison, implementing recommended
      mitigation strategies would cost approximately $4 trillion.
Then the answer relevancy score should be >= 0.7 8334ms FAILED 8334ms 
Error: OpenAI API key is not set. Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or add `apiKey` to the provider config.
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:176

  174 |
  175 | Then('the answer relevancy score should be >= {float}', async ({}, _threshold: number) => {
> 176 |   const result = await matchesLlmRubric(
      |                  ^
  177 |     `The answer must directly address the question: "${ragCtx.question}". It should not contain irrelevant information.`,
  178 |     ragCtx.ragAnswer,
  179 |     {},
Scenario: Retrieved context is relevant to the question@context-precision Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the retrieved context: PASSED 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: 1ms PASSED 1ms 
      Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050 versus $4 trillion for mitigation.
Then the context precision score should be >= 0.6 8330ms FAILED 8330ms 
Error: OpenAI API key is not set. Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or add `apiKey` to the provider config.
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:185

  183 |
  184 | Then('the context precision score should be >= {float}', async ({}, _threshold: number) => {
> 185 |   const result = await matchesLlmRubric(
      |                  ^
  186 |     `The retrieved context must be relevant to answering: "${ragCtx.question}". It should not contain noise or unrelated information.`,
  187 |     ragCtx.retrievedContexts.join('\n'),
  188 |     {},
Scenario: Retrieved context contains needed information@context-recall Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the retrieved context: PASSED 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050 versus $4 trillion for mitigation.
And the reference answer "Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050. Mitigation costs $4 trillion." PASSED Then the context recall score should be >= 0.7 8264ms FAILED 8264ms 
Error: OpenAI API key must be set for similarity comparison
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:194

  192 |
  193 | Then('the context recall score should be >= {float}', async ({}, threshold: number) => {
> 194 |   const result = await matchesSimilarity(ragCtx.ragAnswer, ragCtx.referenceAnswer, threshold);
      |                  ^
  195 |   expect(result.pass).toBe(true);
  196 | });
  197 |
Scenario: RAG answer is factually correct against reference@factual Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the RAG answer: 1ms PASSED 1ms 
      Climate inaction is projected to cost $23 trillion by 2050, while
      implementing mitigation strategies would cost about $4 trillion.
And the reference answer "Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050. Mitigation costs $4 trillion." PASSED Then the factual correctness score should be >= 0.7 8263ms FAILED 8263ms 
Error: OpenAI API key is not set. Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or add `apiKey` to the provider config.
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:199

  197 |
  198 | Then('the factual correctness score should be >= {float}', async ({}, _threshold: number) => {
> 199 |   const result = await matchesFactuality(
      |                  ^
  200 |     ragCtx.question,
  201 |     ragCtx.referenceAnswer,
  202 |     ragCtx.ragAnswer,
